metadata.name: eas-prediction
metadata.summary: Python client sdk for eas inference service

modules.name: eas_prediction.onnx_processor_data_helper
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: eas_prediction.onnx_processor_data_helper.to_array
documentation:
Converts a tensor def object to a numpy array.
Inputs:
    tensor: a OnnxData.TensorProto object.
Returns:
    arr: the converted array.

functions.name: eas_prediction.onnx_processor_data_helper.from_array
documentation:
Converts a numpy array to a tensor def.
Inputs:
    arr: a numpy array.
    name: (optional) the name of the tensor.
Returns:
    tensor_def: the converted tensor def.

functions.name: eas_prediction.onnx_processor_data_helper.convert_endian
documentation:
call to convert endianess of raw data in tensor.
Inputs:
    tensor: OnnxData.TensorProto object to be converted.

modules.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFRequest

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFRequest.set_signature_name
documentation:
Set the signature name of the model
\:param signature_name: signature name of the tensorflow model

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFRequest.add_feed
documentation:
Add input data for the request, a tensorflow model may have many inputs with different
data types, this methods set data for one of the input with the specified name 'input_name'
\:param input_name: name of the input to be set
\:param shape: shape of the input tensor in format of array, such as [1,784]
\:param content_type: type of the input tensor (predefined data type, such as TFRequest.DT_FLOAT)
\:param content: data content of the input tensor, the content could be a single value (e.g. 3.14), or a expanded one-dimension array (e.g. [1,2,3,4,5])
(NOTE: blade.request needs param 'batchsize', while to keep consistent with EAS default TFRequest api, we removed this argument, by deduce the 'batchsize' from shape's first dimention.)

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFRequest.add_fetch
documentation:
Add output node name for the request to get, if not specified, then all the known outputs are fetched,
but for frozen models, the output name must be specified, or else the service would throw exception like:
'Must specify at least one target to fetch or execute.'
\:param output_name: name of the output node to fetch
(NOTE: blade.request output_info has field output_type, while to keep consistent with EAS default TFRequest api, here we chooce to ignore it.)

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFRequest.to_string
documentation:
Serialize the request to string for transmission
\:return: the request data in format of string

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFRequest.parse_response
documentation:
Parse the given response data in string format to the related TFResponse object
\:param response_data: the service response data in string format
\:return: the TFResponse object related the request

Request for tensorflow services whose input data is in format of protobuf,
privide methods to generate the required protobuf object, and serialze it to string

classes.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFResponse

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFResponse.get_tensor_shape
documentation:
Get the shape of a specified output tensor
\:param output_name: name of the output tensor
\:return: the shape in format of list

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_tf_request.TFResponse.get_values
documentation:
Get the value of a specified output tensor
\:param output_name: name of the output tensor
\:return: the content of the output tensor
(NOTE: blade.request output_info has field output_type, while to keep consistent with EAS default TFResponse api, here we choose to ignore it.)
Deserialize the response data to a structured object for users to read

modules.name: eas_prediction.predict_client
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.init
documentation: Initialize the client after the functions used to set the client properties are called

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_endpoint
documentation:
Set the endpoint of the service for the client
\:param endpoint_name: name of the endpoint, such as <http://pai-eas-vpc.cn-shanghai.aliyuncs.com>

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_service_name
documentation:
Set the service name for the client
\:param service_name: name of the service to access

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_endpoint_type
documentation:
Set the endpoint type, support GATEWAY, DIRECT, VIPSERVER, default is GATEWAY
\:param endpoint_type: type of the endpoint

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_token
documentation:
Set the authentication token of the service for the client
\:param token: service token, automatically generated when deploying the service

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_content_type
documentation:
Set the content_type for the client
\:param content_type: content_type

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_log_level
documentation:
Set log level for logging module
\:param log_level: target log level

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_retry_count
documentation:
Set the max count of retrying when an error occurred during a request
\:param count: max retry count

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_max_connection_count
documentation:
Set the max connection count which is the upper limit of connections in the connection pool
\:param count: max connection count

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.set_timeout
documentation:
Set the request timeout for the client
\:param timeout: timeout of a single request
\:return:

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.add_extra_headers
documentation:
Set the request headers for the client
\:param headers: headers of a single request
\:return:

methods.name: eas_prediction.predict_client.PredictClient.predict
documentation:
Perform the prediction request to the server by sending an http request of which the request body
may be in different format (string, protobuf or other user defined format), make it an abstract
class implementing a basic function 'to_string()' to serialized the request to string for sending
\:param req: abstract class of the request
\:return: service response correlated with the input request

Client for accessing prediction service by creating a fixed size connection pool
to perform the request through established persistent connections.

modules.name: eas_prediction.torch_request
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: eas_prediction.torch_request.TorchRequest

methods.name: eas_prediction.torch_request.TorchRequest.add_feed
documentation:
Add input data for the request, a pytorch model may have many inputs with different
data types, this methods set data for one of the input with the specified 'index'
\:param index: name of the input to be set
\:param shape: shape of the input tensor in format of array, such as [1, 3, 224, 224]
\:param content_type: type of the input tensor, can be one of the predefined data type, such as TorchRequest.DT_FLOAT
\:param content: data content of the input tensor, which is expanded to one-dimension array, such as [1,2,3,4,5]

methods.name: eas_prediction.torch_request.TorchRequest.add_feed_map
documentation:
Add input data for the request, a pytorch model may have many inputs with different
data types, this methods set data for one of the input with the specified 'index'
\:param index: name of the input to be set
\:param shape: shape of the input tensor in format of array, such as [1, 3, 224, 224]
\:param content_type: type of the input tensor, can be one of the predefined data type, such as TorchRequest.DT_FLOAT
\:param content: data content of the input tensor, which is expanded to one-dimension array, such as [1,2,3,4,5]

methods.name: eas_prediction.torch_request.TorchRequest.add_fetch
documentation:
Add output index for the request to get, if not specified, then all the known outputs are fetched
\:param index: index of the output node to fetch

methods.name: eas_prediction.torch_request.TorchRequest.to_string
documentation:
Serialize the request to string for transmission
\:return: the request data in format of string

methods.name: eas_prediction.torch_request.TorchRequest.parse_response
documentation:
Parse the given response data in string format to the related TorchResponse object
\:param response_data: the service response data in string format
\:return: the TorchResponse object related the request

Request for pytorch services whose input data is in format of protobuf,
privide methods to generate the required protobuf object, and serialze it to string

methods.name: eas_prediction.test_predict_client.PredictClientTestCase.test_prediction_tensorflow
documentation: def test_prediction_direct(self):
self.client.set_endpoint_type(ENDPOINT_TYPE_DIRECT)
self.client.init()
request = StringRequest('[{}]')
response = self.client.predict(request)

documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: eas_prediction.gateway_endpoint.GatewayEndpoint
documentation:
Endpoint for the gateway access which provide a static http address for
service accessing, there's no need for client to implement round robin,
every time a request is invoked, just return the static http address.

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_request.BladeRequest.add_feed
documentation:
Add input data for the request, a tensorflow model may have many inputs with different
data types, this methods set data for one of the input with the specified name 'input_name'
\:param input_name: name of the input to be set
\:param batchsize: batch size of the input tensor
\:param shape: shape of the input tensor in format of array, such as [1,784]
\:param content_type: type of the input tensor (predefined data type, such as BladeRequest.DT_FLOAT)
\:param content: data content of the input tensor, the content could be a single value (e.g. 3.14), or a expanded one-dimension array (e.g. [1,2,3,4,5])

methods.name: eas_prediction.blade_request.BladeRequest.add_fetch
documentation:
Add output node name for the request to get, if not specified, then all the known outputs are fetched,
but for frozen models, the output name must be specified, or else the service would throw exception like:
'Must specify at least one target to fetch or execute.'
\:param output_name: name of the output node to fetch
\:param output_type: type of the output tensor (predefined data type, e.g BladeRequest.DT_FLOAT)
